Title: Modulating the activity of the APC/C regulator SISAMBA improves the sugar and antioxidant content of tomato fruits

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific APC/C regulator SAMBA; conserved SHR1, LCR, and SHR2 motifs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in floral buds (20 DBA), 1 DBA, and 10 DPA fruit; lower at 20 DPA and red ripe; absent in root; higher in young leaves; strong during embryogenesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in floral buds (20 DBA), 1 DBA, and 10 DPA fruit; lower at 20 DPA and red ripe; absent in root; higher in young leaves; strong during embryogenesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; promoter GUS-GFP; subcellular localization by transient eGFP expression; TurboID proximity labeling; yeast two-hybrid; GC-MS metabolite profiling; LC-MS metabolite profiling; RNA-seq; °Brix measurement
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Sucrose; soluble sugars; phenolic acids; flavonoid glycosides; cyanidin-3-glucoside; kaempferol-3-O-rutinoside
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sugars contribute to fruit taste and economic value; phenolic/flavonoid metabolites contribute antioxidant capacity and potential pathogen/ROS responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SlSAMBA increases fruit soluble solids (°Brix), elevates sucrose-associated sugar status, and increases multiple antioxidant phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolites in tomato fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlSAMBA is a conserved APC/C-associated regulator highly expressed during early flower and fruit development. 2. CRISPR loss of function caused dwarfism, altered ovary/fruit morphology, and reduced fertility. 3. slsamba fruits showed higher °Brix and altered sugar metabolism, with sucrose-associated changes linked to up-regulation of sugar transporters and sucrose metabolism genes. 4. slsamba fruits accumulated higher levels of phenolic acids and flavonoid glycosides at ripening, improving antioxidant profile.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial reduction or precise tuning of SlSAMBA activity could be used to improve tomato sweetness and antioxidant content, but strong loss of function causes severe dwarfism, reduced seed set, and fruit shape changes; dosage editing or allele engineering would be preferable
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf plants, reduced stem diameter and leaf area, smaller flowers, impaired pollen viability/germination, drastically reduced seed number, reduced fruit weight, altered fruit shape
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; fruit developmental stages 3, 5, 8, and 52 DPA are critical for interpretation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA); untargeted primary metabolomics by GC-MS; specialized metabolomics by LC-MS; TurboID proteomics by LC-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SlSAMBA promoter (1.6 kb) used for GUS-GFP reporter; CRISPR/Cas9 promoter details NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SlSAMBA is not a biosynthetic enzyme or transcription factor; it is an APC/C regulatory component affecting nutrient metabolism indirectly through developmental and transcriptional reprogramming. The paper suggests species-specific divergence of SAMBA growth function relative to Arabidopsis.
